audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for fleshly factionalism, planting/watering, God's field/building, foundation, temple, no boasting in humans.

verses.restored: And I, siblings, was not able to speak to you as spiritual ones, but as fleshly-patterned, as infants in Messiah.
I gave you milk to drink, not solid food, for you were not yet able. But not even now are you able,
for you are still fleshly-patterned. For where there is jealousy and strife among you, are you not fleshly-patterned and walking according to human ways?
For when one says, I am of Paul, and another, I of Apollos, are you not merely human?
What then is Apollos? And what is Paul? Servants through whom you trusted, as the Lord gave to each.
I planted, Apollos watered, but the Divine caused growth.
So then neither the one planting is anything nor the one watering, but the Divine causing growth.
The one planting and the one watering are one, and each will receive one's own wage according to one's own labor.
For we are coworkers of the Divine; you are field of the Divine, building of the Divine.
According to the grace of the Divine given to me, like a wise master-builder I laid a foundation, and another builds upon it. But let each watch how one builds upon it.
For no one is able to lay another foundation beside the one being laid, which is Jesus Messiah.
If anyone builds upon the foundation gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, straw,
each one's work will become manifest; for the day will make it clear, because it is unveiled in fire, and the fire itself will test what sort each one's work is.
If anyone's work that was built remains, that one will receive a wage.
If anyone's work is burned up, that one will suffer loss, but that one will be saved/healed, yet so as through fire.
Do you not know that you are temple of the Divine and the Spirit of the Divine dwells in you?
If anyone destroys the temple of the Divine, the Divine will destroy that one; for the temple of the Divine is holy, which you are.
Let no one deceive oneself. If anyone among you thinks oneself wise in this age, let that one become foolish, so that one may become wise.
For the wisdom of this world is foolishness before the Divine; for it is written: He catches the wise in their craftiness.
And again: The Lord knows the reasonings of the wise, that they are empty.
So then let no one boast in humans. For all things are yours:
whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas, or world or life or death, or present things or coming things—all are yours,
and you are Messiah's, and Messiah is the Divine's.

translation_notes: You are temple in verses 16-17 is plural/community-focused; the assembly is the holy dwelling, so division becomes temple-harm.
Fleshly-patterned refers to factional ego-behavior, not the material body as evil.
